quotes.verbatim_quote: Open chords use open strings in their construction.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This is the chapter's core definition — it establishes what distinguishes open chords structurally from closed/barre chord voicings.

quotes.topic: Open chords — key limitations and comping use
quotes.verbatim_quote:
Though they are limited to a few keys, these shapes add new flavor to your comping in
particular key centers. Open chords have a specific sounds that is unique to the guitar.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This passage gives the practical rule for when to deploy open chords (comping in specific guitar-friendly key centers) and names their defining sonic property — unique open-string resonance — which governs their musical use.

This equation defines a load-bearing enharmonic/functional equivalence between two chord symbols for the same voicing, teaching the student how to read and name complex open-string chords from multiple harmonic perspectives.
